text.title: Spec-Driven Debugging Examples
text.summary: End-to-end worked example illustrating the methodology against a realistic input.

example.title: BUG-2026-05-001 orders validation regression
example.note: Symptom: POST /api/orders 200 OK + empty body when total>9999. Expected: 422 + problem+json. Repro minimised to 12-line pytest. bisect identified commit a3f1c8d that removed a guard clause. Fix in b4e2d9f re-adds guard + emits problem+json. regression_test_path=tests/test_orders_validation.py::test_total_over_max; incident_link=GH#142. Bug never returned in subsequent 6 months despite three rewrites of the orders flow.

example.title: Guess-and-fix loop
example.note: Engineer guessed the cause was a typo in a model; reverted; bug remained. Tried 4 more guesses; 2 hours wasted. Tree caught: guess-instead-of-bisect + no-regression-test → use bisect with failing spec as oracle; commit b3e... identified in 4 steps.
